title: stapelia variegata var. retusa
intro: Stapelia variegata var. retusa, also known as the 'Carrion Flower', is a succulent plant species in the family Apocynaceae, characterized by its unique, waxy leaves and striking, star-shaped flowers that resemble rotting flesh to attract carrion-feeding flies for pollination. Traditionally, the Stapelia variegata var. retusa plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Stapelia variegata var. retusa is often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and capsules, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
